tagged_story_for_tts: <[silence:3400ms]>Robots without metal. <[silence:1300ms]> Movement like living tissue.<[silence:1300ms]>"Soft robotics" describes machines built from flexible materials—silicone, rubber, or even fabric—that bend and stretch like muscles instead of clanking like gears.<[silence:1300ms]>If traditional robots are rigid skeletons, soft robots are octopuses—squeezing through cracks, gripping delicate objects, or inflating to lift heavy loads without crushing them.<[silence:1300ms]>Their secret?<[silence:700ms]>Air, fluid, or electric signals make them expand, contract, or wiggle.<[silence:700ms]>A silicone "gripper" might wrap around a strawberry like a finger—no bruising.<[silence:700ms]>A "robot worm" could slither into collapsed buildings to find survivors.<[silence:1300ms]>Harvard’s 2016 "soft robotic glove" used pressurized tubes to help stroke patients re-learn hand movements.<[silence:700ms]>NASA tests inflatable rovers that flatten to land, then puff up to roll over alien terrain.<[silence:1300ms]>No gears to freeze. clean_story: Robots without metal.  Movement like living tissue. "Soft robotics" describes machines built from flexible materials—silicone, rubber, or even fabric—that bend and stretch like muscles instead of clanking like gears. If traditional robots are rigid skeletons, soft robots are octopuses—squeezing through cracks, gripping delicate objects, or inflating to lift heavy loads without crushing them. Their secret?Air, fluid, or electric signals make them expand, contract, or wiggle. A silicone "gripper" might wrap around a strawberry like a finger—no bruising. A "robot worm" could slither into collapsed buildings to find survivors. Harvard’s 2016 "soft robotic glove" used pressurized tubes to help stroke patients re-learn hand movements. NASA tests inflatable rovers that flatten to land, then puff up to roll over alien terrain. No gears to freeze. No joints to jam. Just material that acts like skin, tendon, or even bone—when needed. Three related subjects are bio-inspired design, wearable tech, and shape-memory alloys.
